proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_078 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months in the overall cohort.
feature_057 (3-level ordinal 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict shorter pfs_months.
feature_013 (heavily right-skewed, range 0.12-3622) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict shorter pfs_months.
feature_006 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict shorter pfs_months.
feature_009 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, plausibly an albumin-like marker) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher values predict longer pfs_months.
Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0 in unadjusted comparison.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman correlation of feature_078 with pfs_months is +0.853 (n=50000, p<1e-300). Very strong positive monotone association.
Spearman correlation of feature_057 with pfs_months is -0.386 (p<1e-300). Mean pfs_months by level: 0->4.68, 1->3.49, 2->2.38.
Spearman correlation of feature_013 with pfs_months is -0.223 (p<1e-300). Quartile means descend monotonically: Q1=4.31, Q2=3.93, Q3=3.62, Q4=3.12.
Spearman correlation of feature_006 with pfs_months is -0.134 (p~3.7e-200).
Spearman correlation of feature_009 with pfs_months is +0.118 (p~1.4e-154). Higher feature_009 -> longer PFS.
Mean pfs_months: feature_051=1 -> 3.509 (n=27481), feature_051=0 -> 4.031 (n=22519). Welch t-test difference -0.521 months, p~1.6e-181 in unadjusted comparison.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across the five categories of feature_018 (race: white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across the four categories of feature_045 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).
Patients with feature_018='black' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_018='white'.
Patients with feature_045='uninsured' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_045='private'.
Patients with feature_045='medicaid' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_045='private'.

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA across feature_018 categories: F=0.564, p=0.689. Group means: asian=3.797, black=3.741, hispanic=3.737, other=3.749, white=3.741. No significant variation across race categories.
One-way ANOVA across feature_045 categories: F=0.344, p=0.794. Group means: medicare=3.754, medicaid=3.741, private=3.736, uninsured=3.726. No significant variation.
Mean pfs_months: black=3.741 (n=6064), white=3.741 (n=32378). Difference -0.0003 months, p=0.987. No detectable disparity.
Mean pfs_months: uninsured=3.726 (n=1994), private=3.736 (n=19862). Difference -0.010 months, p=0.831.
Mean pfs_months: medicaid=3.741 (n=7087), private=3.736 (n=19862). Difference +0.005 months, p=0.871.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, log(1+feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, race (feature_018), and insurance (feature_045), feature_078 retains a positive independent association with pfs_months.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_051 loses its negative association with pfs_months because it is confounded by other covariates (notably feature_013).
Patients with feature_051=1 have substantially higher feature_013 levels than feature_051=0, explaining the apparent univariate disadvantage of feature_051=1.
feature_109=1 is associated with longer pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.
feature_039=1 is associated with longer pfs_months after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_051 + feature_009 + feature_109 + feature_039 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS (n=50000, R^2=0.970). Adjusted coefficients: feature_078 +0.174 (p<1e-300); feature_057 -1.151 (p<1e-300); log(1+feature_013) -0.383 (p<1e-300); feature_006 -0.077 (p<1e-300); feature_009 +0.477 (p<1e-300); feature_051 +0.0009 (p=0.808, not significant after adjustment); feature_109 +0.152 (p~1e-185); feature_039 +0.148 (p~3e-178). Race and insurance coefficients all |coef|<0.01 with p>0.5.

analyses.code: Compare univariate beta for feature_051 (=-0.521) with adjusted beta (+0.0009)
analyses.result_summary: Feature_051's coefficient shrinks from -0.521 (univariate) to +0.0009 (multivariable, p=0.808). The univariate effect is fully explained by other covariates.

Mean feature_013 by feature_051: feature_051=0 -> 11.96, feature_051=1 -> 67.83. ~5.7-fold higher feature_013 in feature_051=1 group; this confounds the univariate negative association of feature_051 with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a multiplicative interaction between feature_051 and feature_078 on pfs_months: the slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is smaller (less positive) for patients with feature_051=1.
There is a multiplicative interaction between feature_051 and feature_057 on pfs_months: the negative effect of feature_057 is attenuated in patients with feature_051=1.
There is a multiplicative interaction between feature_051 and log(1+feature_013) on pfs_months: the negative effect of log(1+feature_013) is attenuated in patients with feature_051=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_078 interaction coefficient = -0.00457 (p~7.5e-46). The slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is shallower (by 0.005 mo per unit of feature_078) in feature_051=1 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_057 + feature_078 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:feature_057 interaction = +0.0275 (p~6e-9). The negative effect of feature_057 is slightly attenuated (less negative by 0.028) in feature_051=1.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * log_f013 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051:log_f013 interaction = +0.0106 (p=0.0018). Slight attenuation of feature_013's negative slope in feature_051=1 patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by race (feature_018): there is a feature_051 x feature_018 interaction.
The effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs by insurance (feature_045): there is a feature_051 x feature_045 interaction.
feature_109 is differentially distributed across race categories (feature_018) -- a treatment access disparity.
feature_109 is differentially distributed across insurance categories (feature_045) -- a treatment access disparity.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 * C(feature_018) + ...', data=df).fit() ; Wald p-values on interaction terms.
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 x race interaction terms (vs reference asian): black p=0.137, hispanic p=0.738, other p=0.980, white p=0.457. No significant heterogeneity of feature_051's effect across races.
feature_051 x insurance interaction terms (vs medicaid reference): medicare p=0.264, private p=0.802, uninsured p=0.819. No significant heterogeneity.

Proportion feature_109=1 by race: asian 10.7%, black 9.4%, hispanic 10.0%, other 9.7%, white 10.0%. Chi-square not formally tested but range is narrow (~1.3 percentage points). No clear access disparity.
Proportion feature_109=1 by insurance: medicaid 10.3%, medicare 9.9%, private 9.9%, uninsured 10.9%. Range ~1 percentage point, no apparent disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a feature_078 x feature_057 interaction on pfs_months: the positive slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is smaller for higher levels of feature_057.
There is a feature_078 x log(1+feature_013) interaction on pfs_months: the positive slope of pfs_months on feature_078 is attenuated when feature_013 is high.
There is a feature_057 x log(1+feature_013) interaction on pfs_months: the negative effect of feature_013 is attenuated for higher feature_057 levels.
There is a feature_078 x feature_009 interaction on pfs_months: the protective effect of feature_009 is larger for higher feature_078.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078*feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_078:feature_057 interaction = -0.00965 (p<1e-300). For each 1-level increase in feature_057, the slope of pfs_months on feature_078 decreases by 0.0097 months/unit.
feature_078:log_f013 interaction = -0.00340 (p~5e-151). High feature_013 attenuates the positive feature_078 effect on pfs.
feature_057:log_f013 interaction = +0.0232 (p~4e-34). The negative log_f013 slope is attenuated as feature_057 increases.
feature_078:feature_009 interaction = +0.00433 (p~5e-42). The protective effect of feature_009 is amplified at higher feature_078.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each level of feature_057 (0, 1, 2), log(1+feature_013) remains negatively associated with pfs_months, but the slope differs across strata (steeper at lower feature_057).
Within each level of feature_057, feature_078 remains positively associated with pfs_months but with a smaller slope at higher feature_057.
Mean pfs_months declines monotonically across feature_057 levels (0->1->2).

analyses.code: for lvl in [0,1,2]: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df[df.feature_057==lvl]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified slopes for log_f013: lvl0 (n=17592) = -0.397; lvl1 (n=24971) = -0.384; lvl2 (n=7437) = -0.346. All p<<0.001. Slope is mildly attenuated at higher feature_057.

analyses.code: for lvl in [0,1,2]: stratified ols on feature_078 etc.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified slopes for feature_078: lvl0=0.180, lvl1=0.174, lvl2=0.159. All p<<0.001. Confirms attenuation of feature_078 effect at higher feature_057, mirroring the formal interaction test in iter 6.
Mean pfs_months: feature_057=0 -> 4.681, feature_057=1 -> 3.492, feature_057=2 -> 2.376. Strict monotone decrease; differences highly significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship of log(1+feature_013) with pfs_months is nonlinear: a quadratic term in log(1+feature_013) improves model fit.
Mean pfs_months declines monotonically across feature_013 quartiles (Q1->Q4).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ log_f013 + I(log_f013**2) + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_006 + feature_009', data=df).fit() ; F-test vs linear model.
analyses.result_summary: Adding quadratic term in log(1+feature_013) yields F=29.7 (df=1, ~50000), p~5e-8. Coefficient on (log(1+feature_013))^2 = +0.0045. Statistically significant but tiny in magnitude (curvature is mild).
Mean pfs_months by feature_013 quartile: Q1=4.31, Q2=3.93, Q3=3.62, Q4=3.12. Strict monotone decrease across quartiles.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 and feature_109 act synergistically: there is a positive multiplicative interaction such that pfs_months is much longer in patients with both feature_039=1 AND feature_109=1, beyond their individual main effects.
In the absence of the partner indicator, neither feature_039=1 nor feature_109=1 confers a meaningful pfs_months benefit; the benefit is concentrated in the (1,1) combination.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_039)*C(feature_109) + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients (feature_039 and feature_109 coded 0/1): feature_039 main = -0.0007 (NS); feature_109 main = +0.0007 (NS); feature_039:feature_109 interaction = +1.435 (p<1e-300). Synergy is the dominant effect; main effects collapse once interaction is modeled.
Unadjusted mean pfs by combo: (0,0) n=40435 mean=3.74; (0,1) n=4467 mean=3.67; (1,0) n=4569 mean=3.67; (1,1) n=529 mean=5.22. Only the dual-positive cell shows benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_039 x feature_109 synergy on pfs_months is consistent (not heterogeneous) across race categories of feature_018.
The feature_039 x feature_109 synergy on pfs_months is consistent across insurance categories of feature_045.
The protective effect of feature_009 on pfs_months is consistent across race categories of feature_018.

After fully adjusting for feature_078, feature_057, log(1+feature_013), feature_006, feature_009, feature_051, feature_109, feature_039 and the feature_078 x feature_057 + feature_078 x log_f013 interactions, race (feature_018) and insurance (feature_045) coefficients are jointly null.

analyses.code: for r in races: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039*feature_109 + ...', data=df[df.feature_018==r]).fit() ; collect interaction coef.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_039:feature_109 interaction by race: white +1.428 (p<1e-300, n=32378), hispanic +1.388 (p~7e-226, n=7562), black +1.377 (p~2e-177, n=6064), asian +1.631 (p~7e-140, n=3019), other +1.939 (p~6e-49, n=977). All large and positive; no qualitative heterogeneity, modest variation likely sampling noise.

analyses.code: for ins in insurance_cats: stratified interaction.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_039:feature_109 interaction by insurance: medicare +1.460 (p<1e-300, n=21057), private +1.328 (p<1e-300, n=19862), medicaid +1.594 (p~3e-292, n=7087), uninsured +1.593 (p~6e-93, n=1994). Synergy is replicated in every insurance stratum.

analyses.code: for r in races: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_009 + feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006', data=df[df.feature_018==r]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Stratified feature_009 slope by race: white +0.481, hispanic +0.477, black +0.475, asian +0.475, other +0.415. All p<<1e-50. Slopes are very similar (range ~0.07).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + log_f013 + feature_006 + feature_009 + feature_051 + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_078:feature_057 + feature_078:log_f013 + C(feature_018) + C(feature_045)', data=df).fit() ; R^2 = 0.972.
analyses.result_summary: All four feature_018 (race) and three feature_045 (insurance) coefficients have |coef| < 0.01 and p > 0.45 in the fully adjusted model. Race and insurance contribute nothing measurable to pfs_months once clinical covariates are included. Total R^2 = 0.972.
